title: Score Reconciliation, Waves, Safe Spawns, Pacing, and Difficulty Options · Code Recall
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs score reconciliation, waves, safe spawns, pacing, and difficulty options from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for fair progression and difficulty model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence def…
objectives: Derive score from immutable resolved events and reconcile totals by entity tier, streak, bonus, and run.
Advance only when active, queued, and pending-fragment counts satisfy a declared completion invariant.
Select and verify a respawn zone using predicted hazards, toroidal distance, grace time, fallback, and player communication.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Separate evidence from confidence · Review Difficulty Access
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 5219 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile scoring, waves, spawn safety, pacing, difficulty options, and non-denigrating progression evidence; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native display, device, audio, file, package, and operating-system effects; then cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Offer descriptive presets and independent assistance for speed, lives, damage, aim, controls, cues, motion, and timing.

Inspect the Difficulty Access trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Offer descriptive presets and independent assistance for speed, lives, damage, aim, controls, cues, motion, and timing.
